ip_outcome_note: Card name is Pikipek; PokemonTCG.io confirms nationalPokedexNumbers: [731] and evolvesTo: [Trumbeak]; flavor text matches canonical Pokedex entry. Art depicts a small black-and-white bird with red head marking in tropical foliage — matches Pikipek's published design. Internal Pokemon IP, no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Pikipek is National Pokedex no. 731, a Normal/Flying type introduced in Generation VII (Pokemon Sun & Moon, 2016). It is Alola's early-route bird — the region's equivalent of Pidgey, Hoothoot, or Taillow. Its evolution line is Pikipek -> Trumbeak (level 14) -> Toucannon (level 28). [PokemonTCG.io: swsh4-143; Bulbapedia: Pikipek, via WebSearch snippet]

The Pikipek line has an unusual design arc: Pikipek and Trumbeak are based on woodpeckers, but Toucannon is based on a Toco toucan. This is grounded in real-world phylogenetics — woodpeckers and toucans are both members of the order Piciformes, making the evolution line a nod to actual evolutionary relationships. [Bulbapedia: Pikipek, via WebSearch snippet]
"It may look spindly, but its neck muscles are heavy-duty. It can peck at a tree 16 times per second!" [PokemonTCG.io: swsh4-143]

related_cards.name: Trumbeak
related_cards.note: Stage 1 evolution — same Alola woodpecker line

related_cards.name: Toucannon
related_cards.note: Stage 2 final evolution — design shifts to toucan; Piciformes phylogenetics reference
